thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.56% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Price returned to entry by the scheduled exit, capturing nothing from SNDK's prior-session surge. The momentum that drove the +6.56% open gap did not extend intraday, suggesting the move was already fully priced at open.
risks_observed: SNDK appears eight times in today's book with identical fills — a probable order-deduplication failure that creates unintended 8x concentration in a single name. Scheduled exits with no intraday stop left the position fully exposed to any adverse move during the holding window.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.51% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.

risks_observed: FDS also appears eight times with identical fills, compounding sector exposure in financial-data software. The absence of any profit-target or stop means this position relied entirely on scheduled time to close, with no mechanism to capture or protect against intraday swings.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.

thesis: NVIDIA initiated a 7.79M-share position in Coherent Corp (filed 2026-05-15), a direct institutional endorsement of COHR's role in AI optical networking infrastructure. RSI 63.3 is not overbought, volume is at-average (1.0x) rather than exhausted, suggesting the market is still absorbing the NVIDIA signal rather than having already fully priced it.
outcome_notes: Price returned exactly to entry by the scheduled exit, indicating the NVIDIA filing catalyst generated no further follow-through on trade date — the market had likely absorbed the 13F/Form 4 disclosure over the two days between filing (2026-05-15) and entry (2026-05-17). The scheduled exit enforced a clean close, but the thesis window had already closed before the position was live.
risks_observed: Filing-date lag is the primary risk that materialised: institutional-holding disclosures are backward-looking and often already priced in by the time they surface in a news feed, making the entry timing structurally late. No stop was present, so a gap or adverse intraday move would have run uncapped to the scheduled close.
